title: Permission Audit and Fix
description: Identify and correct insecure file permissions on a web server document root.
problemStatement:
You are hardening a web server. The document root is `/var/www/html`. You discover that some files have overly permissive permissions, allowing any user on the system to modify them.

**Setup (simulate insecure files):**
```bash
sudo mkdir -p /var/www/html
cd /var/www/html
sudo touch index.html config.php
sudo chmod 666 index.html
sudo chmod 646 config.php
```

**Tasks:**
1. List the current permissions of all files in `/var/www/html` using `ls -l`.
2. Identify the security issue: `index.html` is world-writable (666) and `config.php` is group-writable by an unknown group.
3. Correct the permissions to a secure standard:
   - Files should be `644` (owner read/write, group/others read-only).
   - Use a single command with a wildcard to fix all `.html` and `.php` files.
4. Verify the new permissions.
5. Ensure the directory itself has proper permissions (`755`) so the web server user (often `www-data`) can traverse and read.

**Bonus:** Change the group ownership of all files to `www-data` to align with the web server process.
hints: Use `sudo chgrp -R www-data /var/www/html` to fix group ownership.
expectedOutput: All files have 644 permissions, directory has 755, group is www-data.
solutionCode:
ls -l /var/www/html
sudo chmod 644 /var/www/html/*.html /var/www/html/*.php
sudo chmod 755 /var/www/html
sudo chgrp -R www-data /var/www/html
ls -l /var/www/html
